Approximate age of the universe?
What is the P vs NP problem?
600.a: Unsolved CS problem — whether every problem whose solution can be quickly verified can also be quickly solved

600.q: What is the Riemann hypothesis?
600.a: Unsolved conjecture about the distribution of prime numbers and zeros of the Riemann zeta function

600.q: What is Bell's theorem?
600.a: Proves no local hidden variable theory can reproduce all predictions of quantum mechanics

600.q: What is Gödel's incompleteness theorem?
600.a: Any consistent formal system powerful enough for arithmetic cannot prove all truths about itself

600.q: What is the Drake equation?
600.a: A probabilistic argument estimating the number of active extraterrestrial civilizations in the Milky Way

600.q: What is the Schwarzschild radius?
600.a: The radius at which an object becomes a black hole — the size of the event horizon

600.q: What is Euler's identity and why is it remarkable?
600.a: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0 — connects five fundamental mathematical constants in one equation

600.q: What is the Standard Model in physics?
600.a: Theory describing fundamental particles and forces (except gravity) that make up the universe

600.q: What is the Higgs boson?
600.a: The particle that gives other particles mass — confirmed in 2012 at CERN's Large Hadron Collider

600.q: What is dark energy?
600.a: A mysterious force (~68% of universe) driving its accelerating expansion

600.q: What is quantum tunnelling?
600.a: Particles passing through energy barriers that classical physics says they cannot cross

600.q: What is entropy in thermodynamics?
600.a: A measure of disorder in a system — always increases in a closed system (Second Law)

600.q: What is the Chandrasekhar limit?
600.a: ~1.4 solar masses — the maximum mass of a stable white dwarf star

600.q: What is Schrödinger's Cat thought experiment?
600.a: Illustrates quantum superposition — a cat is simultaneously alive and dead until observed
